title: Graduated into a recession with a Mass Communication degree.
caption: University of Lagos, top 3% of the class. Learned to make an argument and hold a room — turns out that's most of product management. [Femi: rewrite in your voice / add the detail only you'd know.]

title: Became someone's first-ever product hire.
caption: Farmcrowdy, Lagos. No playbook, three mobile apps, and 29 Nigerian states I actually travelled to so I'd know who I was building for. [Draft — punch it up.]

title: MasterCard Foundation Scholar.
caption: Post-grad in Technology, Design & Entrepreneurship — the year the tinkering got a name. [Draft.]

title: Joined Paystack, learned how money actually moves.
caption: A Stripe company. Shipped a payment method from a one-line idea to tens of millions in volume, and discovered I like the part where the promise meets the ledger. [Draft.]

title: Ran corporate banking across two continents and five time-zoned PMs.
caption: Nigeria, the UK, and a lot of 6am calls. Grew a team; one mentee ended up running a whole division. [Draft.]

title: Moved to Toronto. Marqeta. Still here.
caption: Lead PM on a card-issuing platform that moves billions. New country, new winter, same habit of building the thing myself to understand it. [Draft.]

title: [The tennis beat — when and why it started]
caption: [Femi: this is yours. When did tennis stop being exercise and become the thing? Keep it specific and a little self-deprecating.]

title: Toronto. Married. Still chasing a clean backhand.
caption: Writing essays for minds that think in spirals, shipping prototypes in the evenings, losing to better players on weekends. [Draft — make it land.]
